pattern: Binary search on the answer combined with a sliding-window count of subarrays with at most k distinct elements.
hints: The uniqueness array has n*(n+1)/2 elements (one per subarray). The median is the element at position ceil(total/2) in sorted order.
Binary search on k (number of distinct elements). Count how many subarrays have at most k distinct elements using a sliding window. If this count >= ceil(total/2), the median is <= k.

The sliding window count function: maintain a frequency map and a left pointer; shrink the window when distinct count exceeds k. Each right position contributes (right - left + 1) subarrays ending at right.

edge_inputs.description: 3 distinct elements - all length-1 subarrays have 1 distinct
single element - uniqueness array has only 1
all same elements - every subarray has 1 distinct
two elements alternating - many subarrays with 2 distinct

stress.description: random arrays with small value range to create repetitions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, max(1, n // 3 + 1)) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def medianOfUniquenessArray(self, nums) -> int:
        # O(n^2) brute force: build all subarray distinct counts, sort, find median
        n = len(nums)
        total = n * (n+1) // 2
        counts = []
        for i in range(n):
            seen = set()
            for j in range(i, n):
                seen.add(nums[j])
                counts.append(len(seen))
        counts.sort()
        # median: (total-1)//2 index (0-based) when total is odd and even (smaller of two)
        return counts[(total-1)//2]
